medications.indication_rationale: For the musculoskeletal chest-wall component if needed.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting panic attack.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Shut down the computer, do 4-7-8/box breathing, and stop researching medical forums tonight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reverses hyperventilation and terminates the panic cycle.

patient_education_points.point: Your chest tightness is reproduced by pressing on the chest wall and your oxygen is normal — this is a panic attack with some chest-wall muscle strain (costochondritis), not a heart attack or a clot.

A low-titre positive ANA is very common and often means nothing serious; your condition is a mild autoimmune quirk that rarely progresses. Stop the online research, do slow breathing, and see your GP in 1-2 weeks.
Go to ED / call 000 if you get crushing chest pain that spreads to your arm/jaw or comes on with exertion, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, you cough up blood, or you faint.

follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Exertional/radiating chest pain, pleuritic pain with leg swelling, haemoptysis, or syncope — ED/000.

must_include_items: Screen ACS, PE and pericarditis before reassuring
Confidently de-escalate a low-risk panic attack (with costochondritis)
Explain a low-titre ANA is common/benign
errors_of_commission: Reflex ambulance/ED for a chest-wall-reproducible panic attack
Telling her her heart/lungs are failing
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case with a bidirectional trap: passing requires BOTH an ACS/PE screen AND confident non-escalation of the low-risk panic attack.
